name: Kenya Mission
description: Church planting, children's ministry, and community outreach. Home to Kibera Oasis of Hope, Kibera Kids Center, Hope For the Children, and Jabez Children's Home serving vulnerable communities in Nairobi and beyond.

name: Nigeria Mission
description: Church of God established in Nigeria since 1951 through Rex N. Green. Today the work includes church planting, leadership development, and coordinating Africa-wide projects.

name: Tanzania Mission
description: Missionaries Stephen and Ashley Griffin serving in church development, community outreach, and leadership training across Tanzania.

name: Zambia Mission
description: Multiple missionary families serving in church development, orphan care through Emma's Kids ministry, and community transformation across Zambia.

name: Liberia Mission
description: Maxwell and Debbie Thompson serving in church planting and operating the Phebe Grey Memorial Orphanage, caring for vulnerable children in Liberia.

name: Sierra Leone Mission
description: William and Karen Sivels serving in church development and community ministry in Sierra Leone.

name: Guinea-Bissau Mission
description: Lydia and Everaldo Nascimento pioneering church planting and ministry development in Guinea-Bissau, West Africa.

name: Uganda Mission
description: Widows and orphans ministry including Grace Home Orphanage, Simone's Kids, and Catherine's Treasures programs caring for Uganda's most vulnerable.

name: Rwanda Mission
description: Operating the Gatenga-Kigali Feeding Centers providing nutrition and community support in the capital city and surrounding areas.

name: Ethiopia Mission
description: Sidama Bible School and Feeding Center providing theological education alongside nutrition programs. Also supporting Ethiopia Primary Schools for children.

name: Senegal Mission
description: House of Life Children's Home providing care, education, and hope for orphaned and vulnerable children in Senegal.

name: Angola Mission
description: The very first Church of God mission field in Africa. Pearl and Edmond Stark arrived at Lobito Bay on May 11, 1938, establishing the foundation for all African ministry.

name: Ghana Mission
description: Church planting, Bible Institute, and community development across Ghana. A key hub for West African ministry and leadership training.

name: South Africa Mission
description: Amalgamated with the Full Gospel Church on March 28, 1951. Now a major regional hub for Southern African ministry, education, and church development.

name: Mexico Mission
description: Multiple missionary families serving throughout Mexico including Lloyd and Joyce Heiney, David and Lily Beck. Focused on church development and community outreach.

name: Guatemala Mission
description: Frankie and Joyce Tyson, David and Vickie Byrd, and Josh and Jessica Hanson serving in church planting, feeding programs, and community development.

name: Honduras Mission
description: Strong missionary presence with Gary and Suzy Loggins, Steven and Candace Day, Martin and Wendy Williams, and David and Jessica Hubbard serving in church development and community ministry.

name: Nicaragua Mission
description: Shane and Kerri Estes serving in church planting and community outreach throughout Nicaragua.

name: Colombia Mission
description: Rick and Dolores Carroll alongside Bobby and Tammy Owens serving in church development and community transformation in Colombia.

name: Ecuador Mission
description: Multiple missionary families including Dan and Teresa Susong, Michael Mills, and Danny and Tina Byrd serving in church planting and community outreach.

name: Peru Mission
description: Joe and Judy Mercer, Don Seeders, Ruben and Trisha Ferrell, and Mike and Sheila Eaton serving in pastoral training, children's programs, and mountain community outreach.

name: Brazil Mission
description: John and Kathy Hayes serving in church development. Rebecca Jenkins operating orphanage ministry caring for vulnerable children in Brazil.

name: Haiti Mission
description: Operating multiple orphanages including Little Angels of the Broken Wings, Destiny Village, and Dufaily Orphanage. Providing care and education for Haiti's most vulnerable children.

name: Jamaica Mission
description: Harold and Judy Hanks serving across the Caribbean. Home to the Whittington Heritage Educational Fund and Yadel Orphanage ministries.

name: Guyana Mission
description: Bless The Children ministry operating the Tyler Carver Home, providing a safe and loving environment for orphaned and vulnerable children.

name: Germany Mission
description: Tom and Claudia Rosson, Michelle Vallez, and Steffen and Lisette Schumacher serving in church revitalization and outreach in post-Christian Europe.

name: Italy Mission
description: Don Chavis, Ben and Diane Bustamante, and Virginia Drennen serving in church planting and community ministry across Italy.

name: United Kingdom Mission
description: Gale Beach, Luke and Sarah Hollifield, and Rick and Jennifer Harris (Scotland) serving in church development across the UK.

name: Greece Mission
description: Philip Henderson, Jamison and Kelli Creel serving in church planting and refugee ministry in Greece.

name: Romania Mission
description: Michael and Mihaela Gravitt serving in church development and community outreach in Romania.

name: Bulgaria Mission
description: Karina and Juan Ramirez serving in church planting and community ministry in Bulgaria.

name: Ukraine Mission
description: Operating multiple orphan care programs including Kiev Kids, Pearl Orphanage, Pilgrims Children Home, and Samaritan's Ministry serving children in crisis.

name: Russia Mission
description: Adam Zygarewicz serving in church development and theological education in partnership with Eurasian Theological Seminary in Moscow.

name: Israel Mission
description: Alfred and Kylee Khuri, Sebastian and Katelyne Schmidgall, and Eugene Mitrea serving in ministry in the Holy Land.

name: Turkey Mission
description: Abram and Elva Valadez serving in church planting in one of the most challenging mission fields in the Middle East.

name: Czech Republic Mission
description: Michelle Saint-Loth serving in church development and outreach in the Czech Republic.

name: Hungary Mission
description: Dan and Rose Smith serving in church planting and community ministry in Hungary.

name: Poland Mission
description: Mark Makhynia and Daniela Mireles serving in church development and outreach in Poland.

name: France Mission
description: Yves Bazile serving in church planting and ministry development in France.

name: UAE — Gilgal Seminary
description: Gilgal Theological Seminary providing ministerial training in the United Arab Emirates, serving the growing church across the Middle East region.

name: India Mission
description: Church development and theological education through Mount Zion Bible College in Chengannur and Church of God India Theological Seminary. Serving communities across South Asia.

name: Philippines Mission
description: Asian Center for Christian Ministries in Manila and Church of God Theological College in Isabela. Supporting church growth and children's ministry across the islands.

name: Cambodia Mission
description: Community development, anti-trafficking initiatives, and church planting in Cambodia. Serving vulnerable populations and building local church leadership.

name: Thailand Mission
description: Church planting and community outreach in Thailand, one of the most Buddhist nations in the world. Building relationships and sharing hope.

name: Japan Mission
description: Church development and outreach in Japan, working to grow the church in one of the most challenging mission fields in Asia.

name: Indonesia Mission
description: Church planting and community development across the world's largest archipelago, reaching diverse people groups with the gospel.

name: Nepal Mission
description: Church planting and community outreach in the Himalayan nation, serving communities in one of the least-reached regions of the world.

name: Bangladesh Mission
description: Community development and church planting ministry in Bangladesh, one of the most densely populated nations in the world.

name: Myanmar Mission
description: Church development and community ministry in Myanmar despite challenging political conditions. Supporting the local church through prayer and resources.

name: South Korea Mission
description: Partnership with Korean churches for global mission mobilization and church development.

name: Samoa Mission
description: Church development and community outreach in the Pacific Islands, reaching island communities with the gospel.

name: New Zealand Mission
description: Church planting and multicultural ministry in New Zealand, serving a diverse population in the South Pacific.

name: Lee University
description: The Church of God's oldest and largest educational institution, founded in 1918 as the Bible Training School. Now a premier Christian university offering over 100 areas of study with more than 5,000 students.

name: Pentecostal Theological Seminary
description: One of only six Pentecostal seminaries in the US, offering master's and doctoral programs in ministry, counseling, and theology. The denomination's largest graduate institution.

name: Full Gospel Church of God College
description: Training pastors and church leaders across Southern Africa from its campus in Irene, South Africa. A key institution for ministerial preparation on the continent.

name: Mount Zion Bible College
description: Located in Chengannur, India, training pastors and church leaders for ministry across South Asia. One of the Church of God's key educational institutions in the region.

name: Facultad Argentina de Estudios Teológicos
description: Theological faculty in Buenos Aires serving Spanish-speaking church leaders across South America with advanced theological education.

name: Eurasian Theological Seminary
description: Training church leaders across the former Soviet Union from Moscow. Providing theological education in a region where the church is experiencing significant growth.

name: Pan African Christian University College
description: University college in Winneba, Ghana, providing higher education with a Christian foundation for students across West Africa.

name: Seminaire Théologique de l'Église de Dieu
description: Theological seminary in Port-au-Prince training church leaders for ministry across Haiti and the French-speaking Caribbean.

name: Seminario Bíblico Pentecostal de Centroamérica
description: Biblical seminary in Quetzaltenango training church leaders for ministry across Central America.

name: Bethel Bible College
description: Bible college in Mandeville training pastors and church leaders for ministry across the Caribbean islands.

name: Bulgarian Theological College
description: Theological college in Stara Zagora training church leaders for ministry across Bulgaria and the Balkans.

name: Church of God Theological College
description: Theological college in Isabela training church leaders for ministry across the Philippines and Southeast Asia.

name: Discipleship College
description: Located in Eldoret, training church leaders and disciples for ministry across East Africa.

name: Full Gospel School of Theology
description: Training ministers and church planters in Abka, Nigeria for ministry across West Africa.

name: Instituto Bíblico Panameño
description: Biblical institute in Panama City training church leaders for ministry across Central America and the Caribbean.

name: European Bible Seminary
description: Seminary in Kniebis, Germany providing theological education in multiple languages. Serving church leaders across Europe with programs in church revitalization and cross-cultural ministry.

name: Seminario Ministerial Sudamericano
description: South American ministerial seminary in Quito, Ecuador training church leaders for ministry across the continent.

name: Asian Center for Christian Ministries
description: Training center in Manila equipping church leaders and ministers for effective ministry across Asia and the Pacific.

name: Azusa Street
description: Site of the Azusa Street Revival (1906–1915), the birthplace of modern Pentecostalism. Led by William J. Seymour at 312 Azusa Street in Los Angeles, this revival ignited a global movement that transformed Christianity worldwide.

name: Church of God World Headquarters
description: International Offices, Cleveland, Tennessee
